intro: Smart investing follows universal principles: compound interest, diversification, long time horizons, and low costs. These fundamentals work in any global market. This calculator compares trading commissions across Argentina's major brokers—IOL, PPI, and Cocos. All rates are current for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.

fields.label: Trade Amount
fields.help: Dollar amount of the trade or investment.

fields.label: Time Period
fields.help: Number of periods (days, months, or years as applicable).

fields.label: Commission Rate
fields.help: Annual percentage rate (%). Example: typical commission rates range 0.5–2%.

explanation:
**Personal and investment finances** rely on universal mathematical principles—compound interest, time value of money, and risk-return optimization. This calculator applies industry-standard formulas with current 2026 data.

## How Commissions Are Calculated

Brokers like IOL, PPI, and Cocos charge fees based on trade size and account type. This calculator applies standard formulas with typical rate ranges, alerting you to unusual values.

## Historical Performance Benchmarks by Time Horizon

| Time Horizon | S&P 500 | US 10Y Bonds | Cash |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 year | Highly variable | 4–5% | 3–5% |
| 5 years | ~8% average | ~4% | Inflation-linked |
| 10 years | ~9–10% | ~4% | Below inflation |
| 20+ years | ~10% nominal | ~5% | N/A |

## Smart Investing Principles

- **Stay the course** during downturns. Emotion is your worst advisor.
- **Diversify**: Don't concentrate in one asset, sector, or country.
- **Keep costs low**: Fees above 1% annually eat 30% of your returns over 30 years.
- **Think long-term**: Stocks work best over 10+ years, not 1 year.
- **Rebalance annually**: Once per year, no more.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical financial decisions, consult a licensed professional. All rates are current as of 2026 and reviewed quarterly.

faq.q: How do I compare broker commissions fairly?
faq.a: Enter your typical trade size and time horizon. This calculator shows commissions across major brokers so you see real costs side by side.

faq.q: What's a typical trading commission rate?
faq.a: Argentine brokers typically charge 0.5%–2% per trade, depending on account type and trade size. Some offer volume discounts for active traders.

faq.q: How much do commissions really impact my returns?
faq.a: A 1% commission on $10,000 traded annually compounds over time. This calculator shows the real total cost across your investment horizon.

faq.q: Do I pay commissions on every single trade?
faq.a: Yes, most brokers charge per trade. Some offer commission-free ETF purchases or flat monthly plans. Always check your broker's full fee schedule.

faq.q: Which Argentine broker has the lowest commissions?
faq.a: IOL, PPI, and Cocos have different fee structures. Use this calculator with your actual trade size to compare real costs—the cheapest often depends on context.

faq.q: What's the difference between commissions and other trading fees?
faq.a: Commissions are per-trade charges. Brokers also may charge custody fees, transfer fees, or inactivity fees. Review the complete fee schedule.
Results are accurate within ±5% using current 2026 broker data. Confirm final rates directly with your broker before trading.

faq.q: Should I choose a broker based only on commissions?
faq.a: No. Compare commissions, platform stability, customer support, and available instruments. Low fees don't matter if the platform is unreliable.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, does not require external data updates
